branding.title: Branding & Appearance
branding.subtitle: Customize the look and feel of the application

arch.title: System Architecture
arch.subtitle: Three-Tier Architecture Overview — ONYX-IX
arch.intro: The ONYX-IX system uses a three-tier architecture that separates presentation, application logic, and data storage for scalability, maintainability, and security. This page provides an interactive reference for understanding each tier, its tools, and their integration roles.

webBrowser.desc: The web browser is the user's primary entry point. It loads Oracle Forms via the Java plugin and renders the interactive system interface.
webBrowser.benefit: Provides rapid access from any network-connected device without additional software installation.

javaJre.desc: Java Runtime Environment 8 runs Oracle Forms as applets inside the browser via the Java plugin.
javaJre.benefit: Provides a unified runtime environment for all system forms and ensures compatibility across Java versions.

posTerminal.desc: The POS terminal handles direct printing and barcode generation in retail stores and distribution centers.
posTerminal.benefit: Integrates with ONYX-IX for processing sales transactions, issuing invoices, and printing barcodes.

weblogic.desc: Oracle WebLogic 10.3.6 is the Java application server that hosts Forms & Reports Servers and manages database connections.
weblogic.benefit: Provides a stable and scalable environment for managing concurrent user loads.

formsReports.desc: Oracle Forms & Reports 11g runs ERP forms and generates dynamic reports from the database.
formsReports.benefit: Forms the core of business logic and transforms raw data into interactive reports.

ordsApex.desc: Oracle REST Data Services (ORDS) and APEX provide a lightweight web interface and REST API services.
ordsApex.benefit: Enables web access without Oracle Forms and simplifies integration with external systems.

reportsServers.desc: The Reports Server generates printed reports (PDF, HTML, Excel) from Oracle Database data.
reportsServers.benefit: Separates data presentation from output format, enabling report customization per requirements.

jdk.desc: Java Development Kit 7 provides the development and compilation tools necessary for Oracle Forms.
jdk.benefit: Provides a development environment compatible with WebLogic 10.3.6 and Forms 11g.

sslHttps.desc: SSL/HTTPS encrypts web connections between the browser and application server to protect sensitive data.
sslHttps.benefit: Ensures transport security and meets governance and compliance requirements.

oracleDb.desc: Oracle Database 21c is the core database that stores all system data and enables complex queries.
oracleDb.benefit: Delivers the highest level of performance, reliability, and security for enterprise data.

oracleLinux.desc: Oracle Linux 8.9 is the operating system that runs the database and application servers with UEK installed.
oracleLinux.benefit: Provides a stable environment optimized for Oracle Database and WebLogic.

step1.title: Open system link and load forms
step1.desc: The user opens the system link in the browser and Oracle Forms load via the Java plugin (JRE).

step2.title: Send request to application server
step2.desc: The browser sends the request via HTTP/HTTPS to the WebLogic server on ERP-IAS.

step3.title: Process business logic
step3.desc: Forms process business logic via Forms & Reports and query the database via TNS/SQL*Net.

step4.title: Return results
step4.desc: Results return from Oracle Database 21c — displayed in the form or generated as a report via the Reports Server.

step5.title: Print at point of sale
step5.desc: For printing: barcode library and direct printing on the POS device.
